name: Attio CRM
what_it_does: Flexible, API-first CRM with a configurable data model and relationship intelligence that tracks contacts, companies, and deals with real-time collaboration and workspace sync.
use_cases: Automatically enrich CRM records with agent-gathered data (news, LinkedIn signals, product usage) by writing attributes via the REST API
Query Attio to identify high-priority leads for agent outreach sequences based on custom scoring attributes the agent has written
Create and update deal records programmatically as an agent progresses prospects through a pipeline based on email or calendar activity
Sync data between Attio and other tools by reading lists and records through the API to power downstream agent workflows
Trigger agent workflows when specific CRM events occur (deal stage changes, attribute updates) via Attio webhooks
not_for: Enterprise sales organizations requiring deep CPQ, forecasting, or ERP integrations out of the box — Salesforce or HubSpot are better fits
Support ticketing or customer success workflows — Attio is focused on sales and relationship intelligence, not support queue management
Teams needing extensive marketing automation built into the CRM — Attio integrates with marketing tools but does not replace them
best_when: You need a highly flexible, API-first CRM where agents and automations are first-class citizens for enriching records, tracking relationships, and triggering workflows.
avoid_when: Your team needs a mature enterprise CRM with deep legacy integrations, advanced forecasting, or large partner ecosystem of out-of-the-box connectors.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 for user-authorized integrations with fine-grained scopes per object type (contacts, companies, deals, notes, tasks). API keys available for server-to-server access. Scopes are read/write separated per resource.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for up to 3 seats with core CRM features; API access included on free plan
pricing.paid_tiers: Plus from $34/user/month; Pro from $69/user/month; Enterprise custom pricing
pricing.notes: API rate limits are higher on paid plans. Free plan allows full API access which is notable for testing agent integrations without commitment.

performance.rate_limits: 200 requests/minute on free; 500 requests/minute on paid plans; rate limit headers returned on all responses

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Attio returns well-structured JSON errors with error codes, field-level validation details, and human-readable messages. Rate limit responses include X-RateLimit-Reset headers. The OpenAPI spec aids agent code generation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Record creation supports checking for existing records by matching attributes before creation, but there are no formal idempotency keys. Agents should query before insert to avoid duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: Attio's flexible data model uses object type slugs and attribute slugs that must be known ahead of time — agents should introspect the workspace schema via the /objects and /attributes endpoints before writing data

Writing to relationship attributes (e.g., linking a contact to a company) requires knowing the internal record ID, not just the name — agents must resolve names to IDs before building relationship payloads
Webhook deliveries use at-least-once semantics — agents receiving Attio webhooks must implement idempotency using the event ID to avoid processing duplicate events
Custom attributes created in the UI may change slugs if renamed — agent code hardcoding attribute slugs will break silently if a workspace admin renames fields
The MCP server provides read/write access to the full workspace — scope it carefully to avoid agents inadvertently modifying or deleting CRM records

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth 2.0 with fine-grained scopes per object type is a strong auth model for CRM data. SOC2 Type II certified. GDPR compliant with EU data residency option. MCP server is third-party maintained — review before use.
